slides.text: A week ago your AI answered questions. This week OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta gave it a job.
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. A full frame high quality photograph, cropped to the top ~70% of the frame, of a cluttered home-office desk at dusk with three laptops angled side by side, each running a different real AI product working through a task on its own — on the left OpenAI's ChatGPT Work grinding through a multi-step to-do list, in the middle Anthropic's Claude Cowork open across the laptop and a phone propped against a coffee mug, on the right Meta's Muse Spark assembling a draft — each showing its real, current branding, nobody at the desk. Along the top edge of the photo, lay the connected reference image Straw_Motion_Logo horizontally, small and subtle in scale — no wider than roughly a third of the frame width — so it stays a quiet brand mark and never competes with the headline or stat callout for attention. Render the logo exactly as provided in the reference image: do not change its shape, proportions, or color in any way, and do not restyle it to match the scene. A soft dark vignette sits behind it for legibility against the photo. Below the photo, filling the bottom ~30% of the frame, is a solid white rounded card sitting on top of the image like a native app UI panel, full width, edge to edge. Inside the card, top-left, is a pill-shaped badge — a fully rounded, stadium-shaped outline with a thin black border and a white fill, matching a simple rounded-rectangle badge — containing the text "Unhypped News" centered inside it, set in Inter font, black text. Render the text inside the pill exactly as "Unhypped News" — capital U, capital N, every other letter lowercase. Never render it in all-caps/uppercase lettering, no matter the surrounding typography style. Positioned next to the pill are three tiny real product logos (OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta) in a row. Below that, in large bold black display type, is the stat callout "3 companies.", and beneath it, in smaller near-black sentence-case text, the supporting line "A week ago your AI answered questions. This week OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta gave it a job." All text on the card — inside the "Unhypped News" pill, the stat callout, and the supporting line — is set in Inter. Clean editorial social media news page layout. Photorealistic, crisp bold typography overlay for the photo, clean flat UI-card typography for the card.

slides.text: For years an AI assistant just meant a chat window — you asked, it answered, and you still did the work.
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. A full frame high quality photograph filling the entire frame edge to edge, of a single laptop on a tidy kitchen table in flat morning light, its screen showing an ordinary, generic AI chat window — a typed question and a plain paragraph answer, nothing else happening, a cursor blinking — a believable everyday chat assistant, not any specific named product; a half-drunk mug of tea beside the trackpad. Along the top edge of the photo, lay the connected reference image Straw_Motion_Logo horizontally, small and subtle in scale — no wider than roughly a third of the frame width — so it stays a quiet brand mark and never competes with the headline or stat callout for attention. Render the logo exactly as provided in the reference image: do not change its shape, proportions, or color in any way, and do not restyle it to match the scene. A soft dark vignette sits behind it for legibility against the photo. Near the bottom of the frame, inset with a visible margin of photo on all sides (not touching the frame edges), floats a compact solid white rounded card, like a notification toast. A soft dark vignette sits behind the card, in the photo, for legibility and separation — in addition to its own subtle drop shadow. Inside the card, top-left, is a pill-shaped badge — a fully rounded, stadium-shaped outline with a thin black border and a white fill, matching a simple rounded-rectangle badge — containing the text "Unhypped News" centered inside it, set in Inter font, black text. Render the text inside the pill exactly as "Unhypped News" — capital U, capital N, every other letter lowercase. Never render it in all-caps/uppercase lettering, no matter the surrounding typography style. Below that, in bold black display type, is the stat callout "Just answers.", and beneath it, in smaller near-black sentence-case text, the supporting line "For years an AI assistant just meant a chat window — you asked, it answered, and you still did the work." All text on the card — inside the "Unhypped News" pill, the stat callout, and the supporting line — is set in Inter. Clean editorial social media news page layout. Photorealistic, crisp bold typography overlay for the photo, clean flat UI-card typography for the card.

slides.text: OpenAI shipped ChatGPT Work, Anthropic shipped Claude Cowork, Meta shipped Muse Spark — same week, same move.
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. A full frame high quality photograph filling nearly the entire frame, of an overhead flat-lay on a raw concrete desk of three phones in a row, each freshly opened to a different real product — OpenAI's ChatGPT Work, Anthropic's Claude Cowork, and Meta's Muse Spark — each showing its real, current logo and name, a finger caught mid-tap opening the middle one. Along the top edge of the photo, lay the connected reference image Straw_Motion_Logo horizontally, small and subtle in scale — no wider than roughly a third of the frame width — so it stays a quiet brand mark and never competes with the headline or stat callout for attention. Render the logo exactly as provided in the reference image: do not change its shape, proportions, or color in any way, and do not restyle it to match the scene. A soft dark vignette sits behind it for legibility against the photo. In the lower-left corner, a small compact solid white rounded rectangle card, not full width, floats over the photo like a small overlay badge. A soft dark vignette sits behind the card, in the photo, for legibility and separation — in addition to its own subtle drop shadow. Inside the compact card, in a single condensed row, is a pill-shaped badge — a fully rounded, stadium-shaped outline with a thin black border and a white fill, matching a simple rounded-rectangle badge — containing the text "Unhypped News" centered inside it, set in Inter font, black text. Render the text inside the pill exactly as "Unhypped News" — capital U, capital N, every other letter lowercase. Never render it in all-caps/uppercase lettering, no matter the surrounding typography style. Positioned next to the pill are three tiny real product logos (OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta) in a row. Directly beneath that row, in bold black display type, is the condensed stat callout "Same week." with the supporting line "OpenAI shipped ChatGPT Work, Anthropic shipped Claude Cowork, Meta shipped Muse Spark — same week, same move." in smaller near-black sentence-case text wrapping beneath it. All text on the card — inside the "Unhypped News" pill, the stat callout, and the supporting line — is set in Inter. Clean editorial social media news page layout. slides.text: Hand ChatGPT Work a real to-do list and it works through the steps on its own, then hands the finished job back.
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. slides.text: The catch: it finishes the task, but you still check it before it ships. Hands-off, not hands-gone.
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. slides.text: Expect the tools you already use to start finishing tasks by default — your inbox drafting its own replies soon.
slides.image_prompt: A vertical viral Instagram news post. A full frame high quality photograph filling nearly the entire frame, of a phone held in one hand over a kitchen counter, its email app showing three reply drafts auto-prepared and waiting in the outbox for a single tap to send — a believable, generic mail interface, not a named product — a breakfast plate and car keys just in frame, early-morning light. slides.text: No hype, no spin — just what actually happened. Follow Straw Motion and we'll track how far hands-off AI really gets.
